friction_items.description: humanities/textual/close_reading.yaml step `declare_step_contract` advertises `tool_humanities_apparatus_audit` as the close-reading-aware gate (presence of line/folio anchors, tradition header, counter-instance section, secondary-criticism ledger), then footnotes 'planned; see docs/ROADMAP.md'. From an auditor's seat, the protocol promises an enforced rigor check that does not exist in `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` or `_HANDLERS`. Manual review against `quality_bar` is the only path — that is invisible to the master audit and bypasses the BLOCKER rollup in `audit_and_validation`. An auditor reading the apparatus has no machine-checkable signal that anchor_density / tradition_declared / counter_instance / edition_pinned / secondary_ledger held. Reproducibility footers are STEM-shaped (RNG seed, software.packages, output.sha256) and do not exist for an apparatus.md.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either ship `tool_humanities_apparatus_audit` in the humanities pack (parser over apparatus.md that grep-checks for ≥1 line/folio anchor per claim, a Tradition: header, a Counter-instance: section, a Secondary criticism: ledger with ≥1 cite), wire it into _router_index.yaml + audit/audit_and_validation, AND remove the 'planned' hedge from close_reading.yaml; or remove the forward reference entirely and tighten the prose to say 'manual review against quality_bar — no machine gate today'.

friction_items.description: `audit/audit_and_validation` runs `audit_assumptions` (Cox PH, missing-data, statistical residuals), `audit_figures` (DPI, .caption.md + .summary.md sidecars, SVG companion), `audit_power`, `audit_evalue`, `preregister_diff`. None of these apply to a humanities essay. The apparatus contract (figure_required: false, table_required: false) auto-waives some, but the protocol still LISTS them as steps the auditor must walk — the auditor either reads ~6 inapplicable sections of audit_report.md OR has to know which to skip. `provenance_completeness` is the worst offender: it demands `.prov.json` sidecars with rng_seed / software.packages / output.sha256 for every output, but `synthesis/paper.md` and `workspace/close_readings/<slug>_apparatus.md` are prose. The auditor opens provenance_audit.md and finds either 0% coverage (BLOCKER barring synthesis) or has to manually carve out humanities outputs from the inventory.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make audit_and_validation domain-aware: read `researcher_config.domain` (humanities|qualitative|engineering|...) and a `step_intent: apparatus` from step_summary.yaml, then skip or replace the inapplicable steps with humanities equivalents (citation_chain_recoverability_audit, edition_pinned_check, dangling_link_count). For provenance, define a humanities sidecar schema: edition / translator / source_archive / shelfmark / accessed_at / archive_url, and have provenance_completeness consume that schema for apparatus / archival outputs.

friction_items.description: TOOL_DEFINITIONS holds 344 tools; audit category alone covers tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_power, tool_audit_assumptions, tool_audit_figure, tool_audit_figure_full, tool_audit_figure_interactivity, tool_audit_figure_coverage, tool_audit_citations, tool_audit_reproducibility, tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_step_literature, tool_audit_findings_query, tool_audit_findings_diff, tool_audit_version_coherence, tool_audit_code_quality, tool_audit_prose, tool_audit_claims, tool_audit_evalue, tool_audit_quality_full, tool_audit_coherence, tool_audit_cliches, tool_audit_cross_deliverable_consistency, tool_audit_reviewer_responses, tool_audit_dashboard_content, plus tool_ground / tool_grounding_verify / tool_grounding_register / tool_ground_from_context / tool_thought_trace / tool_discussion_coverage_audit / tool_claim_verify / tool_preregister_diff / tool_reliability_report. That is ~30 audit-flavored tools. For the humanities scenario the auditor needs maybe 5 (tool_audit_claims, tool_audit_citations, tool_grounding_verify, tool_audit_coherence, tool_audit_quality_full). server.py is 7499 lines. The shortlist must come from sys_active_tools, but that requires already knowing the protocol — chicken-and-egg.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: collapse the figure trio (tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage) into a single tool with a mode= param; collapse grounding (tool_grounding_register + tool_ground_from_context + tool_grounding_verify) into tool_ground with op=register|verify|from_context; promote tool_audit_quality_full as the SINGLE auditor entry point and demote the per-dimension tools to advanced surface (don't ship in list_tools by default; surface via sys_tool_describe on request).

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_completeness returns status='error' when any step has BLOCKERS, and tool_synthesize refuses to assemble. The auditor needs to know WHICH gate fired WHY. The description says it writes workspace/logs/step_completeness.md but the JSON envelope returned to the AI is `{status: error, error: '...'}` — the AI then has to sys_file_read the log to surface the actionable list. Same shape for tool_audit_synthesis ('default-denies when zero PDFs are present') — the override (override_no_pdfs=true + override_rationale) is documented in the description but the error message itself does not name the override. From the auditor's perspective, the gate output is a black box without an extra read-tool call.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Have every default-deny / blocker tool return a structured `blockers: [{code, message, fix_hint, override_param}]` array in the JSON envelope alongside the human-readable error. The AI surfaces those inline without needing a follow-up sys_file_read. Pin override flag names + rationale templates in the structured block.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_literature is built for STEM steps (workspace/<step>/literature/findings_vs_literature.md with `## Claim:` blocks + Verdict lines AGREES|DISAGREES|EXTENDS|DEFERRED + matching Discussion). Humanities close-reading 'literature' is the secondary-criticism ledger inside apparatus.md, not a per-step PDF folder with verdicts. The literature_required: true field in step_summary.yaml flags the gate as required, but the ledger format does not match what the gate parses. Auditor either sees false BLOCKERS or has to override the gate per step.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a humanities branch to tool_audit_step_literature: when step_intent: apparatus + secondary_ledger_path is set in step_summary.yaml, parse the ledger format from close_reading.yaml (named critic + agreement/departure) instead of the AGREES/DISAGREES verdict schema. OR introduce tool_humanities_secondary_ledger_audit as the humanities-side gate, wired into audit_and_validation.

friction_items.description: pre_submission_checklist.yaml step `override_audit_review` reads workspace/logs/override_log.md and flags UNRESOLVED bypasses with rationale not mirrored in cover_letter.md. For a humanities essay the cover_letter format differs from the STEM cover letter; the auditor needs to know whether the cross-check actually applies to humanities outputs. Also: every bypass with `<no rationale provided>` is RED, but the close_reading apparatus contract IS effectively a structured bypass of figure/table gates — there is no concept of 'gate auto-waived by step_intent: apparatus' in the override log, so the apparatus waivers are invisible.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Distinguish AUTO-WAIVED (apparatus intent waives figure_gate) from MANUAL-OVERRIDE (researcher passed override_completeness_gate=true). Auto-waivers go in step_summary.yaml under `gates_waived: [figure_gate: 'apparatus intent']` not in override_log.md; pre_submission_checklist reads both surfaces and rolls them up with different severity bands.

friction_items.description: close_reading.yaml + citation_chains.yaml + humanities_essay_structure.yaml are dense — each protocol's description block is 15-25 lines of literary-critical doctrine before the steps start. Beautiful for a humanities scholar; intimidating for an auditor whose job is to verify rigor, not to interpret de Manian deconstruction. The auditor wants a one-line 'what this protocol promises + what artefacts it produces' header.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a `summary` field (already used in _router_index.yaml entries) to each protocol's top matter that distills the description to ~3 sentences for the auditor. Render `summary` first in sys_protocol_get; collapse the doctrine into a `rationale` block the auditor can skip.

friction_items.description: Three audit protocols in protocols/audit/ (audit_and_validation, pre_submission_checklist, provenance_completeness) plus the master tool tool_audit_quality_full plus discussion_coverage_audit. From the auditor's seat the question is which to run when — the cross-references in audit_and_validation point at synthesis, provenance, reproducibility; the cross-references in provenance_completeness point at audit_and_validation; pre_submission_checklist runs LAST. The chain is correct but undocumented as a single visual flow.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add `audit_flow.md` to docs/ showing the audit DAG (provenance → audit_and_validation → discussion_coverage → pre_submission_checklist) with which artefacts each consumes/produces. Or generate it from the next_protocol chains.

friction_items.description: Humanities pack only ships 3 tools (archive_lookup, transcribe, citation_chain) and they are scaffold writers, not actionable artefacts the auditor can verify. Critical-criticism ledger / dangling-link audit / edition-pinning audit are absent. The 7 humanities protocols (close_reading, distant_reading, archival_research, source_provenance, citation_chains, hermeneutic_method, digital_humanities_workflow, scholarly_edition) outnumber the tools 2:1 — the audit gates lean entirely on the generic STEM-flavored tools.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Ship 2-3 humanities-side audit tools as part of Phase 6/7: tool_humanities_apparatus_audit (already promised), tool_humanities_dangling_link_audit (parses citation chain documents for `as quoted in` links + verifies recoverability), tool_humanities_edition_pinning_audit (every primary-text quote has an edition cite).

convoluted_tools: tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage / tool_audit_figure_interactivity — four near-identical figure auditors; an auditor cannot tell at a glance which is the full one
tool_grounding_register / tool_ground / tool_ground_from_context / tool_grounding_verify — three register-flavored tools + one verify; the noun-overload (ground / grounding) is confusing

tool_audit_quality_full vs tool_audit_step_completeness vs tool_audit_step_literature — overlapping scopes; tool_audit_quality_full ALREADY chains the others but they are still listed as standalone in the protocol descriptions, so the auditor sees them twice
tool_thought_trace lives in `memory` category but is described as an auditor surface in pre_submission_checklist — category mismatch hurts discoverability
tool_claim_verify (CoVe) vs tool_audit_claims vs tool_grounding_verify — three claim-checking surfaces with different semantics; the relationship is not stated anywhere
confusing_protocols: humanities/textual/close_reading — references tool_humanities_apparatus_audit which does not exist; auditor cannot tell whether the rigor gate is enforced
audit/audit_and_validation — steps 1 (audit_master) and steps 2-8 (individual audits) overlap; the protocol says step 1 is PREFERRED but still walks the AI through 7 more component steps
audit/provenance_completeness — STEM-centric sidecar schema (rng_seed / sha256 / software.packages) applied uniformly to humanities prose outputs; the protocol does not call out humanities exemptions
audit/pre_submission_checklist — long (10 steps, ~340 lines); the auditor reads it as a checklist but it reads more like a doctrine essay

humanities/synthesis/humanities_essay_structure — the no_imrad_headings quality bar collides with synthesis_paper's IMRAD enforcement; the protocol notes the conflict but the resolution chain (this → synthesis_paper for final audit) is fragile
recommendations: Phase 6 humanities pack: ship tool_humanities_apparatus_audit, tool_humanities_dangling_link_audit, tool_humanities_edition_pinning_audit; remove the 'planned' hedge in close_reading.yaml.
Phase 7 audit consolidation: make audit_and_validation domain-aware by reading researcher_config.domain + step_intent; auto-skip STEM-only steps for humanities; surface a humanities-equivalent set.

Phase 9 tool collapse: merge tool_audit_figure_* into one tool with mode=basic|full|coverage|interactivity; merge tool_grounding_* into tool_ground with op=...; promote tool_audit_quality_full as the auditor entry tool.
Phase 8 envelope upgrade: every default-deny tool returns structured blockers: [{code, message, fix_hint, override_param}] so the AI surfaces actionable failures without sys_file_read.
Phase 5 protocol polish: add a `summary` (3-sentence) field to every protocol top matter; render it FIRST in sys_protocol_get so the auditor sees the verdict-shape before the doctrine.

Define a humanities provenance sidecar schema (edition / translator / source_archive / shelfmark / archive_url / accessed_at) and route provenance_completeness through it for apparatus + archival outputs.
Distinguish auto-waived (step_intent: apparatus → gates_waived in step_summary.yaml) from manual-override (override_log.md). pre_submission_checklist consumes both with different severity bands.
Generate docs/audit_flow.md from next_protocol chains so the auditor can see the audit DAG at a glance.
